results.title: Copenhagen holiday
results.brief_description: A film whose feature is the Bing and Grohdahl Porcelain Potteries in Copenhagen. Shows the beautiful ceramic figures being made and hand-painted. The sung narration adds an unusual slant to the film.

results.title: A Conversation with Frank Lloyd Wright
results.brief_description: One of the world’s most original and influential architects discusses his theories of functional architecture and analyses contemporary styles. In a conversation covering a broad range of topics, Mr Wright displays the agile wit and eloquence that have made him one of the most colourful personalities of our time.

results.title: Concrete pays
results.creator_credit: British Information Services and Drayton Film Productions
results.brief_description: The use of concrete can help farmers achieve greater efficiency, safety and hygiene. Deals with its use on farms for roads, yards and block-walling, insulated floors and the correct methods to be used in such work by inexperienced farm labour. Uses of cement based paints are also shown.

results.title: Concorde
results.brief_description: Concerned with the background to Concorde, in particular some of the problems which occurred when designing and building an airliner for supersonic flight. Covers various aspects of the problem including: pioneering problems, airframe stresses, design, temperature problem, structure and assembly. Contains some animated sequences. Narrated by Nick Reid.

results.title: Concorde gives up smoking
results.creator_credit: Aerospatiale France and British Aircraft Corporation
results.brief_description: Production models of Concorde will have smoke-free engines. The imporvements made are illustrated by the comparison of production line engines with the prototypes.

results.title: Community health: the Australian concept
results.creator_credit: John Sexton, Suzanne Baker and Film Australia
results.brief_description: Produced for the Hospitals and Health Services Commission, the film illustrates current developments in the area of community health in Australia, paying particular attention to the new initiatives and trends introduced by the Health agencies.

results.title: Commonwealth Review. No. 21
results.brief_description: Cinemagazine containing the following items: Ice breakers at work in the Gulf of St Lawrence; Asian students in New Zealand under the Colombo Plan; Adventure training for girls at Kenya’s Outward Bound School; Commonwealth students assist with testing spectographic analysis machines; The centenary celebrations of the Ballygunge Cricket Club in Calcutta; The Commonwealth stand at the Schoolboy’s Exhibition in London.

results.title: The Commandos
results.creator_credit: Geoffrey Thompson, Australia. Royal Air Force. Films Advisory Bureau and Cine Service
results.brief_description: Shows the tough training which makes CMF commandos: unarmed defence, cross-country fitness tests, cliff-climbing, parachute-jumping, canoeing and swimming. Includes an exercise in which commandos attach an “enemy strong-point” by sea and air.

results.title: Colored Nabeshima ware
results.brief_description: The early Japanese learned from the Koreans how to fire and glaze white ceramics, and the art of color glazing from the Chinese. Introduction of these advanced techniques from the Asian continent helped the Japanese to develop their own methods and styles in ceramic art, including those of the uniquely Japanese vessels, the colored Nabeshima ware.
